sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides. extremely calorie dense by default because that is the unavoidable category burden. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal.

closing.summary: Lard stands out for highly useful in cooking and efficient energy source. but it also gets held back by extremely calorie dense by default and easy to overpour without noticing. Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.

closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
